Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action (Forum Non Conveniens)
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Oakfabco, Inc.'s motion to dismiss the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens.

APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; Christopher C. Ranck, Esq. of Selman Breitman LLP 979-2060 for deft & moving party Oakfabco, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified - denied without prejudice. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Nebraska is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) Defendant's alternate motion to stay the action is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation remain in California. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)
